message:
test(fcp-conformance): add file-preopen + (second) rejection tests to sandbox isolation suite

Extends `sandbox_isolation_conformance.rs` with additional fail-closed
coverage on the WASI preopen surface. The earlier
`wasi_symlinked_preopens_fail_closed` commit proved that symlinks
pointing into unexpected directories are rejected; this commit adds
the orthogonal case: preopen paths pointing at regular files rather
than directories.

**`wasi_file_preopens_fail_closed`** (new test):

Creates a real temp directory (`fcp-conformance-sandbox-file-preopen`)
and places two regular files inside it (`readonly.txt`, `writable.txt`).
Tries to hand each file path to `WasiConfig::readonly_paths` and
`WasiConfig::writable_paths` respectively, and asserts that in both
cases:

- `WasiRuntime::new(...)` succeeds (the config itself is syntactically
  valid — the failure comes at store-creation time, not config-
  construction time).
- `runtime.create_store()` fails with an error whose `to_string()`
  contains `"must be directories"`.

That is the exact wording the WASI runtime layer emits when it
refuses to treat a non-directory preopen as a filesystem root. The
test pins both the rejection and the error message so downstream
callers relying on substring-matching the error for UX purposes
(for example, an operator CLI that formats the message into a
friendlier banner) keep working.

Why this matters as conformance surface, not just a unit test:
preopen misconfiguration is a real-world operator mistake, and a
WASI runtime that silently accepted a file path and gave the
sandboxed guest something that looked like a directory with one
entry would create a confusing and subtle capability leak (a
guest's fd_readdir on what it thinks is a directory would see one
file, a guest write could stomp or extend the file, etc.). Failing
closed with a diagnostic-grade message is the correct behavior
and this test locks it in place.

The second sibling test added in this hunk (body follows the same
`assert! … contains(...)` shape) exercises a related rejection path
— see the diff for specifics — on the same preopen surface.
